THIS project's conventions, not just generic quality. Edit the prose freely; the "Encoded Policy"

# Review Policy — {project-name}

How a reviewer — human, or the `code-reviewer` agent — must review changes in this codebase.
Most structural rules are auto-enforced by the pre-commit gate + sensors (see the last section);
a reviewer's leverage is on what sensors cannot judge: correctness, domain-rule fidelity, and
readability.

{review-policy}

## What to reject

- A change that violates the import hierarchy or a bounded-context edge above — the layer and
  context gates block these at commit; flag any attempt to route around them.
- A test that reaches a real external system instead of the boundary-doubles kit — the
  `live-externals` sensor blocks this; scrutinize any `harness:live-ok` override that is not a
  genuine, isolated staging smoke test.
- A new secret-shaped literal on a non-fixture line — secret-scan blocks this; scrutinize any
  `harness:secret-ok` override.
- Business logic that contradicts the ubiquitous-language terms in `specs/design/CONTEXT.md`.

## What the sensors already enforce (do not re-litigate)

Lint / type / format, function & file length, layer imports, coupling & cycles, mutation-smoke,
the coverage ratchet, secret scan, and the regression suite all run automatically at commit and
gate time. Restating those in review wastes the reviewer's attention — spend it on domain
correctness, edge cases, and design instead.
